content:
TENDER FOR “SERVICE CONTRACT FOR RADIANT TUBE SUPPORT
MODIFICATION AND ENHANCEMENT OF HEAT DUTY FOR VDU
HEATER AT BPCL MR”.
_________________________________________________________________________
PAYMENT TERMS:-
For Heater Revamp Job as per the Line Item
a. 20% against delivery of material at site
b. 20% against completion of Pre TA activities.
c. 40% against completion of jobs during TA
d. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of the
unit.
e. 5% against submission of documents
For Heater Radiant Tube Replacement Job as per the Line Item
a. 30% against completion of Pre TA activities.
b. 50% against completion of installation during TA
c. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of the
unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
For Heater Convection Tube Replacement Job as per the Line Item
a. 30% against completion of Pre TA activities.
b. 50% against completion of installation during TA
c. 15% against successful completion of the heater revamp jobs and startup of the
unit.
d. 5% against submission of documents
For other SOR items in the contract payment against submission of the RA Bills as
per tender condition.
Payment will be on a pro-rata basis as per the mutually agreed billing schedule by
vendor & BPCL prepared after the award of PO.
CONTRACT DURATION & COMPLETION PERIOD:
The start date of the job will be the PO date and the PO will be valid for one year period.
The subject heater revamp is planned during the turnaround of CDU3 unit in the month of
April 2026 with an expected start date of 16th April 2026.
The shutdown duration will be for 30 days with available engineering days of 22 for the
heater revamp job. The engineering days will govern as the time of completion for this
contract.
The pre-shutdown activity of this job is planned to start from 20th of Feb 2026 and the
contractor should mobilise the required manpower and resources for the same.
breadcrumb: TENDER FOR “SERVICE CONTRACT FOR RADIANT TUBE SUPPORT MODIFICATION AND ENHANCEMENT OF HEAT DUTY FOR VDU > CONTRACT DURATION & COMPLETION PERIOD

content:
All the material in the scope of supply of the contractor should reach the worksite latest by
15th Feb 2026.
LIQUIDATED DAMAGES FOR DELAY:
Considering criticality of the jobs involved, delay in completion of the turnaround jobs will
lead to production loss and hence revenue loss to the corporation. Thus, timely completion of
turnaround jobs is important. Accordingly, following LD clause is proposed to be included in
package contracts tenders.
In case the contractor fails to complete the mechanical work within the stipulated period, he
shall be liable to pay liquidated damages of 1% of the total contract value of contract per day
and or part thereof of the delay, that the work may remain incomplete as per the time
schedule, subject to a maximum of 5% of the total contract value.
WARRANTY/DEFECT LIABILTY:
The supplier shall have final and total responsibility for the design, material, manufacturing,
workmanship, and performance of the revamped heater including all equipment’s and
associated accessories supplied by the vendor and shall provide warranty against defective
design, material and workmanship for a duration of 12 months from the date of
commissioning of heater at BPCL MR or 18 months from the date of receipt of the latest
material in the package, whichever is earlier.
Any defects observed during the warranty period shall be rectified by the vendor in a time-
bound manner at their own cost. Vendor shall submit Performance Bank Guarantee for the
contract, which shall be at 5% of the basic value of the contract valid from the date of receipt
of all the supply components of the heater package at site and up to 12 months from the date
of successful commissioning of the heater plus 6 months claim period.
GUARANTEE
Contractor shall guarantee revamped portion of heater components / parts against defective
design and materials, poor workmanship and failure from normal usage for a period as
specified in tender document / GPC. Supplier shall repair or replace, without charge any
defective parts found within that time. This guarantee shall also include sub ordered/
subcontracted / bought-out items forming part of the total contractor supplies. If a separate
guarantee clause has been specified in procurement specification for items in Contractor
scope of supply, then the stringent of the two shall govern.
Contractor shall guarantee to accept full responsibility for all fabricated components / parts of
the revamped portion of heater system in Contractor's scope of supply / fabricated by him in
regard to selection, fabrication and coordination of the various components.
Any damage caused to existing heaters / FIMs during execution of the job by Contractor shall
be rectified without no cost or time implication to Client

content:
PENALTY FOR NON-MOBILIZATION OF KEY CONSTRUCTION MANPOWER
Penalty for non-mobilization per day per person after the contractual mobilization
Period unless agreed otherwise by the Engineer-in-Charge:

Rs. 3000/- per day per person for Resident construction manager/ Resident
Engineer/ Site in- charge.

Rs. 1000/- per day per person for Site engineer/ Site supervisor/ Safety
Supervisor/Planning Engineer/Billing engineer.
Notes: (for Penalty clauses)

All intervening off days (Sundays etc.) and holidays will be counted for levy
of
Penalty.

Mobilized personnel shall not be demobilized till contractual completion or
based on consent of Engineer-in-Charge else penalties as above shall be
applied,

The above penalties are over & above all other contractual provisions for late
Mobilization of resources.
SECURITY DEPOSIT TOWARDS PERFORMANCE / RETENTION MONEY /
PBG:-
PBG is applicable if total contract value including taxes is more than 10 lakhs.
Refer clause 18.1 of the General Conditions of Contract (GCC), the content mentioned in 2nd
para i.e. "an Account payee Demand Draft payable to BPCL or" is deleted.
In lieu of security deposit towards performance / retention money, Bidder shall submit bank
guarantee (BG) of equivalent amount of retention money (5% of contract value) before
submission of 1st RA bill. The Bank Guarantee shall be valid and remain in force till the
contractual completion period with a defect liability period of twelve months and with a
claim period of six months thereafter. Such BG can be for 5% of the contract value for the
performance guarantee period, upfront, or for 5% value of each running bill valid for the
performance guarantee period.
PBG as applicable shall be collected within 15 days from the award of the contract.
However, where the vendor opts for performance security in the form of retention money
from the running bills, in such case the vendor shall be required to submit PBG of 2.5% of
the total contract value within 15 days from the award of the contract and the remaining 2.5%
shall be subsequently deducted from each bill.
In case the successful Bidder is not furnishing the performance security deposit as referred
above on award of the job, the same shall be deducted from each running account bills at the
rate of 5% of bill value till overall security deposit of 5% as mentioned above is collected.
The security deposit will be retained till the successful completion of the work and thereafter
till the expiry of the defect liability period (refer clause-74), if applicable. This retention
money / Bank guarantee held shall be released after the expiry of the defect liability period

content:
provided that any defects appearing during that period are corrected by the contractor and
subject to Clause 18.2 of GCC.
The Bank guarantee if submitted shall be from any Indian scheduled bank or an international
bank of repute having a branch in India or a corresponding banking relationship with an
Indian scheduled bank. The security deposit / retention money shall be in Indian Rupee in the
case of domestic bidders and in US Dollars / EUROS in the case of foreign bidders.
Process for submitting Bank Guarantee / PBG under SFMS (Structured Financial Messaging
System) mode as follows:-
Vendor shall insist their bank for issuance of SFMS Bank Guarantee for faster payments.
Vendor shall provide BPCL's Bank Account No. & IFSC Code (Details given below) to their
Bank as beneficiary at the time of application for Bank Guarantee in favor of BPCL. Issuing
Bank shall issue the Bank Guarantee & send SFMS message to BPCL's Bank confirming the
authenticity of Bank Guarantee who in turn shall send the confirmation to BPCL. Vendor
should ensure the following for issue of E-bank guarantee.
a. The issuing bank is on SFMS platform.
b. SFMS Message type used is 760 COV and SFMS Delivery report / Message copy is sent
along with original BG.
c. For BG amendment, message type 767 COV is to be used.
d. SFMS contains following details:
i. Beneficiary's bank name : ICICI Bank
ii. IFSC Code : ICIC0000393
iii. BPCL's Customer ID : BPCL583493800
e. BG Issuing bank should send the BG issuance advice through SFMS to BPCL's
designated banker : ICICI Bank, Backbay Branch, Mumbai (IFSC: ICIC0000393)
f. BG Issuance advice should mention applicable Unique Identifier Code (UIC) in row /
field number 7037 of SFMS Delivery Report.
a. BPCL Location : Kharghar, Navi Mumbai
b. Head Office : Ballard Estate
c. UIC : BPCL583493800
g. The original BG should be submitted along with print out of SFMS Delivery report from
the BG Issuing Bank Branch.
h.SFMS BG will help in faster verification of BGs and prompt release of payment to vendors.
TRADE RECEIVABLES DISCOUNTING SYSTEM (TReDS):-
breadcrumb: TENDER FOR “SERVICE CONTRACT FOR RADIANT TUBE SUPPORT MODIFICATION AND ENHANCEMENT OF HEAT DUTY FOR VDU > SECURITY DEPOSIT TOWARDS PERFORMANCE / RETENTION MONEY /

content:
Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) is an institutional mechanism set up in order
to facilitate discounting of trade receivables of MSMEs from corporate buyers through
invoice discounting by multiple financiers. Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) is
registered with TReDS platform of the aggregators M/s. Receivables Exchange of India Ltd
(RXIL), M/s Invoice mart, M/s. M1xchange, M/s C2treds, M/s DTX . The eligible MSME
bidders can avail the discounting facility by registering either in one or multiple TReDS
platform of the aggregators. It enables the sellers (MSMEs) to discount their invoices through
the aggregators to the financiers at competitive rates thus unlocking their working capital
swiftly.
